---
title: "Alt_LOH"
author: "Robert Reid"
date: "`r Sys.Date()`"
output: html_document
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
library(data.table)
library(pracma)
library(ggplot2)
library(Matrix)
library(dplyr)
library(stringr)
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = TRUE)
```

Load functions

```{r}
source("chimera_functions.R")
```

File locations

```{r}
read_data_path = '/Users/breid/Dropbox/__Work/__Lab-Notebook/2025/00 RAD5-OE-consolidated/Read Analysis/read_data_chromosome/RAD5_01/full.csv.gz'
SNP_data_path = '/Users/breid/Dropbox/__Work/__Lab-Notebook/2025/00 RAD5-OE-consolidated/Read Analysis/sk50_calls_nrp017_filtered.vcf.gz'
Chrom_data_path = "/Users/breid/Dropbox/__Work/__Lab-Notebook/Genome/new_ref_v0.1/S288C_genome_v0.1.0.fasta.fai"
```

Load

```{r}
read_data = load_read_data(read_data_path)
SNP_data = load_snp_data(SNP_data_path)
chrom_data = load_chr_size(Chrom_data_path)
```

```{r}
  # ── 2. Filter reads and classify alleles ─────────────────────────────────────
  message("  Filtering reads and classifying alleles ...")
  full_read <- read_data[
    mapq      >= 55L  &
#    base_qual >= 5L & # removing base quality filter seems to give fewer singletons
    is_del    == 0
  ]

  full_read <- merge(
    full_read, SNP_data,
    by.x  = c("chrom", "pos"),
    by.y  = c("CHROM", "POS"),
    all.x = TRUE
  )

  full_read[, IS_REF := call == REF]
  full_read[, ALLELE := fcase(
    call == REF, "REF",
    call == ALT, "ALT",
    default = "OTHER"
  )]
  full_read <- full_read[ALLELE != "OTHER"]
  full_read <- full_read[, .(chrom, pos, read_id, IS_REF, ALLELE)]
  setorder(full_read, read_id, pos)

  # Snapshot of all filtered reads BEFORE chimeric-only subsetting.
  # Returned as $full_read so the caller can build a population-level LOH map
  # that covers fixed-haplotype regions even where no chimeric reads exist.
  full_read_all <- copy(full_read)

```

Allele balance

```{r}
summary_dt <- full_read_all[
  ,
  .(
    REF = sum(IS_REF),
    DEPTH = .N,
    balance = sum(IS_REF) / .N
  ),
  by = .(chrom, pos)
][order(chrom, pos)]
```

Cluster with mixed model?

```{r}
library(mclust)

fit <- Mclust(summary_dt$balance, G=3)

summary(fit)

summary_dt[, cluster := fit$classification][]

# Get cluster means
cluster_means <- data.table(
  cluster = seq_along(fit$parameters$mean),
  mean_balance = fit$parameters$mean
)

cluster_means[
  order(mean_balance),
  AC := 0:2
]

summary_dt[
  cluster_means,
  AC := i.AC,
  on = "cluster"
][]


# maybe instead of all this below, we just ignore single cluster changes unless
# there is very good evidence.
#
# AC = 0 has an allele balance < 0.02
# AC = 1 has an allele balance > 0.4 & < 0.6
# AC = 2 has an allele balance > 0.98

#cluster_assignments <- gmm_model$classification
#
#plot(gmm_model, what = "classification")

### Examine posteriors

# Assuming your mclust fit is called 'fit' and your AB vector is 'allele_balance'

#z <- fit$z  # posterior probability matrix (n_sites × 3)
#colnames(z) <- c("Cluster1", "Cluster2", "Cluster3")
#
## Find sites near 0 or 1 that got assigned to cluster 2
#
#problem_sites <- which(
#  fit$classification == 2 & (summary_dt$balance < 0.13 | summary_dt$balance > 0.87)
#)
#
## Inspect their posteriors
#z[problem_sites, ]
#summary_dt[problem_sites, ]

# this isn't working
# seems to pull out random stuff
```


LOH runs using data.table functions

```{r}
# First-pass run labeling
run_map <- copy(summary_dt)
run_map[, LOH_factor := rleid(AC), by = chrom]
run_map[, AC_runs := .N, by = .(chrom, LOH_factor)]

# For short runs, apply strict balance filter — remove rows that fail
run_map <- run_map[
  !(AC_runs <= 2 & !(
    (AC == 0 & balance < 0.02) |
    (AC == 1 & balance > 0.4 & balance < 0.6) |
    (AC == 2 & balance > 0.98)
  ))
]

# Re-label runs after deletions so formerly-interrupted runs merge
run_map[, LOH_factor := rleid(AC), by = chrom]
run_map[, AC_runs := .N, by = .(chrom, LOH_factor)]

LOH_table <- run_map[
  ,
  .(
    start = min(pos),
    end = max(pos),
    length = max(pos) - min(pos) + 1,
    AC = unique(AC),
    AC_runs = unique(AC_runs),
    balance_mean = mean(balance, na.rm = TRUE),
    balance_sd = sd(balance, na.rm = TRUE)
  ),
  by = .(chrom, LOH_factor)
][
  ,
  allele := fcase(
    AC == 0, "SK1",
    AC == 2, "S288C",
    default = "het"
  )
]
```


LOH table produced

Plot


Load CEN positions from file

```{r echo=FALSE,message=FALSE,warning=FALSE,results='hide'}
CEN <- readr::read_delim("../../../Dropbox/__Work/__Lab-Notebook/Genome/new_ref_v0.1/CEN.txt", delim = "\t", 
                      escape_double = FALSE,
                      col_names = c("CHROM","SGD","element","start","end","dot1","dot2","dot3","ID_info"), 
                      trim_ws = TRUE) %>%
  dplyr::select(c(CHROM,start,end,ID_info)) %>%
  dplyr::mutate(chromosome = factor(stringr::str_remove(CHROM,"chr"),
                             levels=as.character(as.roman(1:16))))
```


Plot & save SNP map

```{r}
#library(dplyr,stringr)
LOH_table |>
#  filter(!stringr::str_detect(chrom,"chrmt")) |>
  dplyr::mutate(chromosome = factor(stringr::str_remove(chrom,"S288C_chr"),
                             levels=as.character(as.roman(1:16)))) |>
  ggplot(aes(xmin=start/1000,xmax=end/1000,ymin=-0.1,ymax=0.1,fill=allele)) +
  geom_rect() +
  facet_grid(chromosome ~ .) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = c("S288C"="dodgerblue","het"="grey","SK1"="firebrick")) +
#  ggtitle(params$sample) +
  theme(
    legend.title = element_blank(),
    legend.position = "none",
    axis.title.y = element_blank(),
    axis.title.x = element_blank(),
    axis.text.y = element_blank(),
    axis.ticks.y = element_blank(),
    axis.text.x = element_text(size = 12),
    strip.background.y = element_rect(fill = "white",colour = "white"),
    strip.text.y = element_text(size = 10,face = "bold",angle = 0),
    panel.border = element_rect(linewidth = 0),
    panel.spacing.y = unit(0.5, "lines"),
    panel.grid.major.y = element_blank(),
    panel.grid.minor.y = element_blank(),
    panel.spacing = unit(-1, "lines"),
    panel.grid = element_line(color = "black",linewidth = 0.1,linetype = "15")
  ) +
  geom_point(data = CEN,aes(x=start/1000,y=0),color="black",pch="•",size=10, inherit.aes = FALSE)  
```
